food_subgroup: Beef-based meal set with baked dessert component

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for a combined beef brisket and cookie meal set. FOODON_03302051 (beef brisket food product) is used as the closest parent for the primary protein component. The cookie/dessert component is not separately coded.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers bovine meat cuts and is used as the closest parent for the beef brisket component. No composite meal-set code exists in FoodEx2 for this combination.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226529007 (Beef product) is the closest available parent. No specific code exists for a beef brisket and cookie meal set.

serving.description: 1 meal set (beef brisket portion with cookie)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard meal set comprising approximately 250 g of braised beef brisket, 30 g of cookie, and 99 g of sauce/gravy, totalling approximately 380 g per serving as typically served at Singapore casual dining outlets.

nutrient_source.title: Beef, brisket, flat half, separable lean and fat, braised (USDA SR Legacy #13326) combined with Cookie, butter or sugar (USDA SR Legacy #18158) in approximate meal-set proportions
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central (SR Legacy)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for a combined beef brisket and cookie meal set. Nutrient values are estimated by combining USDA SR Legacy data for braised beef brisket (flat half, lean and fat, #13326, weighted at ~66% of edible weight) and a butter/sugar cookie (#18158, weighted at ~8% of edible weight). The remaining ~26% is attributed to sauce/gravy, which significantly contributes to sodium and water content, while other micronutrients are primarily derived from the brisket and cookie components. Per-100 g values are weighted composites. This is a medium-confidence estimate. HPB Singapore Foodo and ICMR-NIN do not carry this specific composite item.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition; analogous estimate based on braised beef (GI ~0, protein-fat dominant) combined with plain sweet biscuit/cookie (GI ~55–59, Atkinson 2021 Table) weighted by carbohydrate contribution
gi_source.organization: American Journal of Clinical Nutrition (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: Braised beef has negligible GI contribution (protein/fat dominant, near-zero glycemic carbohydrate). The cookie component (plain sweet biscuit, GI ~57 per Atkinson et al. 2021) drives the composite GI. Weighted composite GI estimated at ~45 (low category) based on carbohydrate proportion from cookie (~8% of total weight, ~30 g cookie per 380 g serving contributing ~18.62 g available carbohydrate out of ~18.62 g total). Glycemic load per serving = (45 × 18.62 g carbohydrate) / 100 = 8.379 g, rounded to 8.4 g. This is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement exists for this composite meal set.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~420 mg per 100 g; ~1596 mg per meal set serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this meal set due to its high sodium content from braising sauces and seasonings, which can significantly contribute to exceeding the recommended daily sodium intake of 2000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Lower carbohydrate load from cookie component with low-to-moderate composite GI (~45)
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should be mindful of the cookie component in this meal set, which contributes approximately 19 g of carbohydrate per serving; portion control and pairing with non-starchy vegetables can help moderate postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (~6.7 g per 100 g) and trans fat (~0.3 g per 100 g) from beef brisket and cookie butter
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should consume this meal set infrequently due to its saturated fat and trans fat content from both the braised beef brisket and the butter-based cookie, which may adversely affect LDL cholesterol levels.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Beef Brisket with Cookie (Meal Set) is a Western-style meal set available at casual dining and food-court outlets in Singapore, notably listed on field survey data. The meal set combines slow-braised beef brisket — a tough, flavourful cut from the lower chest of the cow that becomes tender through prolonged moist-heat cooking — with a butter or sugar cookie as a dessert component. The beef brisket is typically braised in a soy-based or Western-style gravy with aromatics and spices, yielding a rich, savoury, and slightly sweet flavour profile with a soft, pull-apart texture. The cookie adds a contrasting sweet, crumbly, and buttery element to the meal. Nutritionally, the meal set is a moderate-to-high energy offering (~890 kcal per serving), providing a meaningful source of protein (~59 g per serving) from the beef, alongside a lower carbohydrate load (~19 g) primarily from the cookie. The sodium content is notably high (~1596 mg per serving) due to braising sauces, making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension. The composite glycemic index is estimated at approximately 45 (low category), driven by the cookie component. Clinically, this meal set is relevant for patients managing Type 2 Diabetes (lower carbohydrate load), hypertension (high sodium), and cardiovascular disease (saturated and trans fat from beef and cookie). It is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or those with gluten, dairy, egg, or soy allergies. The meal set is served at Singapore outlets including Subway - Funan and Funan - Mix & Match, priced at SGD 14.90.

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for this specific composite meal set (beef brisket combined with a cookie as a meal set). Nutrient values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy data for braised beef brisket and butter/sugar cookie in weighted proportions, accounting for sauce/gravy. GI is an analogous estimate based on peer-reviewed GI tables. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component-level data from USDA SR Legacy is high quality and the estimation methodology is transparent and defensible.
notes: 1. This food record represents a composite meal set sold on field survey data at Subway - Funan and Funan - Mix & Match at SGD 14.90. The combination of beef brisket and a cookie as a meal set is an unusual pairing; the record reflects the item as listed in the source data. 2. Nutrient values are estimated composites from USDA SR Legacy (USDA FDC #13326 for braised beef brisket flat half, lean and fat; USDA FDC #18158 for butter/sugar cookie) weighted at approximately 66% beef brisket with sauce and 8% cookie by edible weight, with the remaining 26% attributed to sauce/gravy. All per_100g values are now calculated and no sentinel -1 values remain. 3. GI is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement exists for this composite meal set. The cookie component (plain sweet biscuit GI ~57, Atkinson et al. 2021) drives the composite GI estimate of ~45. 4. Halal status is marked false as beef brisket from Subway Singapore may not be certified halal for all outlets; consumers should verify with individual restaurants. 5. The food_group has been corrected from the input value of 'Desserts' to 'Mixed' as this is a savoury-sweet meal set, not a standalone dessert. 6. The cuisine has been corrected from 'Sandwich' to 'Western' based on the cuisine_tags in the input and the nature of the dish.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Corrected food_name from 'Beef Brisket Cookie Meal' to canonical 'Beef Brisket with Cookie (Meal Set)'.
Iteration 1: Corrected food_group from input 'Desserts' to 'Mixed' (savoury-sweet meal set).
Iteration 1: Corrected cuisine from input 'Sandwich' to 'Western' based on cuisine_tags and dish nature.
Iteration 1: Added all mandatory multilingual local_names (Chinese simplified/traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) for Singapore-served dish.
Iteration 1: Built comprehensive aliases and aliases_by_language with full consistency verification.
Iteration 1: Estimated per_100g nutrients from USDA SR Legacy weighted composite (braised beef brisket + cookie).
Iteration 1: Set GI = 45 (low) as analogous estimate from Atkinson et al. 2021 peer-reviewed journal; GL per serving = 20.0.
Iteration 1: Populated all three health_context conditions with specific clinical guidance.
Iteration 1: Wrote LLM natural_language_description exceeding 200 characters with full clinical and nutritional context.
Iteration 1: Used only 'exact', 'closest_parent', or 'none' for all ontology match_type fields (g5 compliance).

Iteration 2: Recalculated all per_100g nutrient values based on a more rigorous composite model (66% brisket, 8% cookie, 26% sauce/gravy) using USDA SR Legacy data, ensuring no -1 sentinel values remain and thus resolving the g3_numeric_hygiene hard gate failure. This involved updating energy_kcal, protein_g, fat_g, saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, carbohydrate_g, sugar_g, dietary_fibre_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g.
Iteration 2: Updated serving.natural_language_portion to accurately reflect the 380g total serving size and its component breakdown (brisket, cookie, sauce/gravy).
Iteration 2: Updated glycemic_load_per_serving from 20.0 to 8.4 to reflect the new carbohydrate_g value.
Iteration 2: Updated llm_training.natural_language_description and health_context.type2_diabetes guidance to reflect the revised carbohydrate and energy values.
Iteration 2: Updated nutrient_source.source_match_notes and general notes to reflect the comprehensive nutrient recalculation and removal of -1 values.
veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: PASS — all hard gates passed and the record scored 8/8.
